steps.title: Identify the appositive structure
steps.content: The phrase “the International Criminal Police Organization” is an appositive noun phrase that explains what “Interpol” is. It was introduced with an opening em dash after “Interpol”. The adverbial phrase “in 1971” follows it.

steps.title: Apply the em dash rule
steps.content:
An appositive phrase introduced by an em dash must be closed with a matching em dash.

Choice A (Organization—) correctly closes it: “Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization—in 1971”.

Choices B, C, and D fail to do this:
  • B uses a colon instead of a closing em dash
  • C provides no closing punctuation
  • D uses a comma, which mismatches the opening em dash


That matches choice A — A is correct.
